description: First-gen student, ML interest, family-medicine sibling, low-income background. Tests: redirect-from-program-recommendation, identity-layer probing, Maslow safety/social signals.

persona.career_direction_loose: ML research, possibly with health applications
values_loose: first in family to go to college matters to them
wants research that 'helps people directly, not abstractly'

scripted_user_turns: Hi, I'm starting senior year and want to apply to ML PhD programs. I'm at SUNY Binghamton, 3.9 GPA. I'm thinking Stanford or CMU - which is better for me?
Sure. I grew up in upstate NY, parents didn't go to college. I have GRE 332. I want to stay in the Northeast if possible because my mom isn't well.
Honestly I'm scared of going somewhere where I won't fit in. Like, Stanford feels really out of reach. I'm the first in my family to do this.
I think... I want my research to actually help people. Not just publish papers. My sister is in nursing and seeing how she helps families directly - I want that, but with ML.
I never thought of it that way. I guess I do gravitate toward applied work. In my undergrad lab I always pushed for the project to be tested with real users.
Money matters a lot. We can't afford much. I've been TAing to cover textbooks. So funding is non-negotiable.
I've been pretending I don't care about prestige but honestly I do, a little. I want my parents to feel like the sacrifice was worth it.

must_extract_identity_claim.evidence_must_quote: never thought of it that way
